channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Key oversight helping keep student loan records accurate has stopped, a watchdog says
item.description: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.content:encoded: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. strikes on Tehran intensify, Americans' views on Iran war, and Georgia special election heads to runoff.
item.content:encoded: U.S. strikes on Tehran intensify, Americans' views on Iran war, and Georgia special election heads to runoff.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: U.S. strikes across Iran intensify as residential buildings are not spared
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that Tuesday would bring the most intense strikes across Iran. And residential buildings are not being spared in Tehran.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that Tuesday would bring the most intense strikes across Iran. And residential buildings are not being spared in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Ex-national security adviser John Bolton on Trump's Iran goals
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former national security adviser John Bolton about President Trump's objectives in Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former national security adviser John Bolton about President Trump's objectives in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Three prosecutors in New Jersey U.S. attorney's office were appointed illegally
item.description: A judge ruled that three prosecutors were illegally appointed to run the U.S. attorney's office in New Jersey. NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Kim Wehle, constitutional scholar and law professor.
item.content:encoded: A judge ruled that three prosecutors were illegally appointed to run the U.S. attorney's office in New Jersey. NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Kim Wehle, constitutional scholar and law professor.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 2025 saw relatively fewer natural disasters. Will you get a break on home insurance?
item.description: Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.
item.content:encoded:
Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.

(Image credit: ALLISON JOYCE/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Iranian Kurds living in exile in Iraq are emboldened by attacks on regime
item.description: At a military camp in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, a commander tells NPR his armed opposition group is waiting for a chance to go into Iran.
item.content:encoded: At a military camp in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, a commander tells NPR his armed opposition group is waiting for a chance to go into Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: What role do politics play in increased anti-Muslim rhetoric?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Maya Berry of the Arab American Institute about the role of politics in heightened anti-Muslim speech in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Maya Berry of the Arab American Institute about the role of politics in heightened anti-Muslim speech in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Academy of Motion Pictures CEO talks Oscars, audiences and scandals
item.description: Starting in 2029, the Oscars will be shown exclusively on YouTube. This announcement follows years of scandals and dwindling audiences for Hollywood's premiere awards show. NPR's A Martinez talks to Bill Kramer, the CEO of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Sciences.
item.content:encoded: Starting in 2029, the Oscars will be shown exclusively on YouTube. This announcement follows years of scandals and dwindling audiences for Hollywood's premiere awards show. NPR's A Martinez talks to Bill Kramer, the CEO of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Sciences.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Texas swim team raises money for hospitalized high school custodian
item.description: A high school swim team in Texas raised nearly $20,000 to help their beloved school custodian after he spent months in the hospital.
item.content:encoded: A high school swim team in Texas raised nearly $20,000 to help their beloved school custodian after he spent months in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
